question: What is the main purpose of Vue.js?

choices: To pass data from parent to child component
correct_answer: To pass data from parent to child component

question: What is the role of ref() in Vue 3?
Which Vue directive is used for two-way data binding in forms?
What is computed() mainly used for in Vue?

choices: To send data from child to parent

question: Why is a unique :key important inside v-for?
choices: Because Vue uses it to track list items efficiently
correct_answer: Because Vue uses it to track list items efficiently

question: What problem does Pinia solve in a Vue application?
choices: It manages shared state between components
correct_answer: It manages shared state between components

question: When is watch() useful in Vue?
choices: When you need to react to changes in reactive data
When you want to create routes
When you need to display static text
When you want to replace props
correct_answer: When you need to react to changes in reactive data

question: What is the role of onMounted() in Vue 3?
choices: It runs code after the component is mounted to the DOM
correct_answer: It runs code after the component is mounted to the DOM

question: Why do developers split Vue apps into multiple components?
choices: To make the code reusable and easier to maintain
To make the app offline automatically
correct_answer: To make the code reusable and easier to maintain

question: What is the main difference between local state and global state in Vue?
choices: Local state belongs to one component, global state is shared across the app
Global state only works in CSS
Local state is always stored in JSON
correct_answer: Local state belongs to one component, global state is shared across the app
